items.title: What are the issues affecting young Chileans ahead of the country's presidential run-off? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Chile goes to the polls on Sunday in a run-off presidential election, with candidates from the political extremes going head-to-head. Jose Antonio Kast of the far-right Republican Party is the frontrunner. He is running on a platform of strict immigration controls, tax cuts, and family values. His principal opponent is Communist Jeannette Jara, the former Minister of Labor and Social Provision. She is advocating for reform and social justice. Both candidates have promised to crackdown on a wave of violent crime.

Chapter Breakdown
0:00 DW's Jacinta Molina reports on how young people in Chile feel about the candidates running for president
3:26 John Bartlett, Journalist in Santiago de Chile, discusses the presidential candidates' election platforms


#chileelections #jeannettejara #kastpresidente

items.title: Kim Jong Un welcomed back soldiers fighting for Russia in Ukraine in a special ceremony | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>North Korean leader Kim Jong Un has welcomed back soldiers returning from fighting for Russia in Ukraine. In a special ceremony in Pyongyang Kim called the soldiers "heroic." 
He acknowledged that nine members of the unit had been killed. In 2024, Moscow and Pyongyang signed a strategic partnership promising military assistance should one of them be attacked. South Korean intelligence says Pyongyang has sent up to 15-thousand troops to support Russia's invasion of Ukraine and that thousands have been killed. 

0:00 North Korea welcomes back troops that return from fighting for Russia in Ukraine
0:36 Jenny Town, Director of the Stimson Center’s 38 North Program 

#northkorea #ukraine 


For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: After the United States agreed to lift sanctions on potash 123 prisoners were let go | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Among those released was prominent opposition leader Maria Kolesnikova. She was one of dozens of political prisoners freed near the Belarus-Ukraine border. In neighboring Lithuania, Nobel peace prize winner Ales Bialiatski was let go at the US embassy in the capital Vilnius. Authoritarian leader Alexander Lukashenko agreed to the pardons as part of a US-brokered deal.

00:00 Belarus releases 123 political prisoners

01:18 Konstantin Eggert, DW Eastern Europe and Russia analyst

05:31 Hanna Liubakova, Belarusian journalist and political analyst with the Atlantic Council’s Eurasia Center

10:20 Katia Glod, Belarus and Russia expert at the New Eurasian Strategies Centre and Center for European Policy Analysis

items.title: How many more waves of attacks can Ukraine's power system endure? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>In Ukraine thousands of people are facing major blackouts after another night of intense Russian attacks.  

The southern port city of Odesa and its power grid came under particularly heavy fire. The government in Kyiv says Russia launched hundreds of drones and at least 30 ballistic missiles overnight. Elsewhere, at least two people were reported killed in Russian drone attacks.

It's just the latest of many Russian strikes on Ukraine's energy infrastructure. DW correspondent Nick Connolly has been to a power station put out of action by an earlier strike. 

#ukraine #russia #ukrainewar 

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Gazans are dying from winter floods | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>'Winter storms are flooding Gaza’s tent camps, killing at least 14 people in a single day. Aid groups warn that too little assistance is reaching the war-ravaged territory, leaving more than a million displaced Palestinians exposed to cold, water and danger as the ceasefire stalls.'

items.title: Half a million people displaced by the border dispute during the last week | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Thailand's prime minister Anutin Charnvirakul says no ceasefire deal has been reached with Cambodia - despite Donald Trump announcing the two sides have agreed to end hostilities. As fighting continues, Cambodia says it is closing all border crossings with Thailand. More than half a million people have been displaced during a week of fighting in a long-running border dispute. DW's Asia Pacific Bureau Chief Georg Matthes visited the border region of Kantharalak where residents were forced to flee the fighting. 


For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Many obstacles blocking phase two of the ceasefire deal between Israel and Hamas | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>The repatriation of all hostages, living and dead, was a key provision of the ceasefire between Israel and Hamas that took effect weeks ago. It has now become a key point of contention. Hamas says it has been unable to retrieve some of the remains from the rubble of Gaza. Today, one hostage's body still hasn't been returned. DW's Tania Krämer met members of his family who described their anxious wait.

0:00 Netanyahu says next phase of the Gaza peace plan is close
3:29 Yossi Mekelberg, Middle East expert, Chatham House, London

items.title: Football idol Lionel Messi's tour of India gets off to a chaotic start | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Football superstar Lionel Messi has kicked off his much-anticipated tour of India in Kolkata. Thousands of excited fans secured tickets to get a glimpse of their idol at the city's Salt Lake stadium, but events took an unexpected turn. Many fans vandalized the football stadium after failing to get a glimpse of their hero before he abruptly left the stadium.

0:00 Terrible event planning angers football fans in Kolkata
3:03 Debayan Sen, Sports journalist

items.title: The ideology behind the US policy on crypto and digital money | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>As global confrontation around trade picks up, we take a look at the strength the United States has in financial markets. Special crypto tokens called stablecoins have emerged as a tool to advance dollar dominance -- a position some in US President Donald Trump's administration have taken. The US position towards crypto differs from many other countries around the world. So how did they get there? We take a deep dive into the Heritage Foundation's Project 2025 blueprint for a second Trump term and untangle the ideological roots of the administration's thinking. To get there, we travel back in time to how money worked in the 19th century in the United States and how offshore dollar markets emerged in the late 1940s in London. Because the battlefields of money will play out about how much public backing each market segment will get in the future.

For more on free banking, the offshore dollar universe and stablecoins, we suggest the following reading:
- "The Suffolk Bank and the Panic of 1837: How a Private Bank Acted as a Lender-of-Last-Resort" by Arthur J. Rolnick, Warren E. Weber and Bruce D. Smith: https://www.minneapolisfed.org/research/working-papers/the-suffolk-bank-and-the-panic-of-1837-how-a-private-bank-acted-as-a-lenderoflastresort
- "On par: A Money View of stablecoins" by Iñaki Aldasoro, Perry Mehrling and Daniel H Neilson: https://www.bis.org/publ/work1146.htm
- "The Geopolitics of Swap Lines" by John Michael Cassetta: https://www.hks.harvard.edu/centers/mrcbg/publications/awp/awp181

CHAPTERS
00:00 Intro
00:19 What is free banking
01:17 Why Stablecoins are similar to free banking
02:30 How Stablecoins strengthen dollar dominance
04:20 Why a lender of last resort is crucial
06:24 The vast domain of offshore dollar
07:52 How swap lines backstopped the offshore dollar world
09:56 Stablecoins lack a lender of last resort
10:45 The United States' unique path towards digital currencies

#dollardominance #crypto #stablecoins #dollar #federalreserve #project2025
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: What is the situation like in the Democratic Republic of the Congo? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>The United States has accused Rwanda of violating a U.S.-brokered peace agreement in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, warning that Rwanda is pushing the region into war. The issue was the subject of an emergency meeting of the UN Security Council, which was called after Rwandan-backed M23 rebels advanced further into the mineral-rich Great Lakes region.

Chapters:
0:00 US accuses Rwanda of breaking Congo ceasefire
3:03 Sella Oneko, DW Correspondent

items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Preparations are underway for another round of talks aimed at ending the war in Ukraine. Earlier this month, U.S. President Donald Trump’s special envoy, Steve Witkoff, was in Moscow. items.title: What exactly do these images show that hasn't been seen before? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>U.S. House Democrats released additional photos showing Donald Trump and other influential figures with the late sex offender, Jeffrey Epstein. The president is pictured with Epstein and a group of women whose faces have been obscured. Other images show Epstein with Bill Clinton. Also pictured are Bill Gates; Britain's former Prince Andrew; Woody Allen; and Trump's former aide, Steve Bannon. It's unclear when or where the photos were taken. The photos released by the Democrats are only a small part of the more than 95,000 images found in Epstein's estate.

Chapters:
0:00 Newly released photos show Trump and Epstein
1:08 Elana Schor, Semafor
5:05 Janelle Dumalaon, DW Correspondent
